top_signals.title: The Golden Age of IPOs is Here: IPOX's Schuster
top_signals.summary:
Josef Schuster, founder and CEO of IPOX Schuster, joins Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Deals." SpaceX is offering 555.6 million shares at a fixed price of $135 each, which would raise about $75 billion. The stock is set to start trading on June 12. The IPO is expected to rank as the biggest ever, topping Saudi Aramco’s $29.4 billion listing in 2019.

 (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: ‘They got very lucky,’ Trump says of downed Apache helicopter’s crew
top_signals.summary: After a U.S. Army Apache helicopter was downed by an Iranian drone, President Donald Trump said the rescued aviators “got very lucky.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Microsoft patches Exchange Server zero-day exploited in attacks
top_signals.summary: Microsoft has patched an actively exploited Exchange Server vulnerability that allows threat actors to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks targeting Outlook Web Access users. [...]
